description: Settled top-level task list for the project. Number of tasks calibrated by project tier (Trivial 1–3, Simpler 3–8, Serious 8–20, Complex 20–30).

tasks.description: Ordered list of task entries. Each task is a vertical slice that calls out what the relevant roles each deliver — not a separate task per role.

task.description: Plain prose describing the task. Lead with the user-facing outcome, then the key deliverables or acceptance criteria. No code, no framework names, no headings.

examples.title: Top-level task list for the project

tasks.task: Scaffold the monorepo shell so every later feature has somewhere to land. Devops: workspace layout, containerised dev environment, CI workflow that runs lint and a placeholder test suite, /health endpoint. Backend: app skeleton with health route, config loading, DB connection bootstrap. Frontend: app shell with empty landing route, build pipeline producing a static bundle the dev container serves.

Add user registration and authentication end-to-end. Backend: register / login / logout / refresh API endpoints, password hashing, session storage. Frontend: signup form, login form, post-signup landing, session-aware nav, error states. Devops: session-store container if separate from the main DB.

Build the raffle cycle and dashboard. Backend: cycle management API, winner selection, automatic rollover. Frontend: dashboard with countdown, ticket history. Devops: scheduling infra for cycle rollover.
